symptoms.symptom_name: Post-exertional collapse in energy, sleep-unrefreshed, triggered by over-exertion 2 days prior

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Q fever fatigue syndrome — post-exertional exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Post-exertional malaise with unrefreshing rest on a known post-infectious background is the hallmark of a QFS/post-viral fatigue crash.
A gradual 2-day exertion-triggered build is not the sudden-onset pattern of acute stroke.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse symmetric deep myalgia across major muscle compartments

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Q fever fatigue syndrome — post-exertional exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse myalgia is characteristic of post-viral fatigue crashes and of the objective distress in QFS.
Prominent objective diffuse myalgia points to a physiological, not primarily depressive, process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cognitive slowing / 'brain fog' with slowed, quiet speech

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Q fever fatigue syndrome — post-exertional exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cognitive dysfunction ('brain fog') and slowed speech are core features of post-viral fatigue crashes.
New speech/cognitive change mandates active exclusion of a focal neurological event even though the overall picture is symmetric.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetric global weakness with NO focal/lateralising deficit (no facial droop, no unilateral weakness, non-slurred speech)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of any focal/lateralising deficit on carer/video screen substantially lowers the probability of an acute focal stroke and is the key discriminator enabling an outpatient disposition.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Q fever fatigue syndrome — post-exertional exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A symmetric, global pattern is consistent with a systemic fatigue crash rather than a focal lesion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile with no new breathlessness or chest pain (pertinent negative for acute infection relapse / endocarditis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile with no cardiorespiratory features lowers acute probability, but does not exclude chronic Q fever endocarditis — echo/serology still required in longitudinal care.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reassuring patient-obtainable vitals — BP 110/70, HR 68, SpO2 98%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal BP and HR without tachycardia or hypotension make significant circulatory compromise less likely at this moment, though home readings are unverified and intake remains poor.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Q fever fatigue syndrome — post-exertional exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Haemodynamic stability is consistent with a non-emergency fatigue crash and supports a home-based disposition with safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced oral intake with low urine output; bedbound and fully dependent on carer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced intake and low urine output on a background of total dependence make dehydration an active acute risk and management target.

symptoms.symptom_name: Documented acute Q fever (C. burnetii) infection ~2 years ago; agricultural/rural occupation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Q fever fatigue syndrome — post-exertional exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prior C. burnetii infection is the aetiological substrate for Q fever fatigue syndrome and for the chronic Q fever endocarditis differential.
A past Q fever infection is the necessary risk factor for chronic Q fever endocarditis — mandates longitudinal cardiac surveillance.

symptoms.symptom_name: Neurological examination — intact cranial nerves, symmetric tone/power/reflexes, no focal deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal focused neurological examination excludes an acute focal stroke — a clinician-only finding sealed here; telehealth must instead rely on the carer/video symmetry screen plus safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cardiac auscultation — no new murmur; normal peripheral perfusion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal auscultation with no new murmur reduces acute endocarditis probability — a clinician-only finding sealed here; telehealth cannot obtain it, so endocarditis stays a longitudinal work-up and safety-net item.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse tenderness to deep palpation across major muscle compartments (quadriceps, deltoids)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Q fever fatigue syndrome — post-exertional exacerbation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective diffuse muscle tenderness corroborates the reported myalgia — a clinician-only palpation finding sealed here; the patient-reported muscle pain remains available on the presentation side.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Q fever fatigue syndrome — post-exertional exacerbation
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: No single feature is diagnostic, but the combination — exertion-triggered sleep-unrefreshed collapse + diffuse symmetric myalgia + cognitive slowing + a symmetric non-focal picture, on a documented post-Q-fever background — constitutes a recognisable QFS crash and, together with reassuring vitals, supports an outpatient disposition with safety-netting rather than emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum electrolytes, urea, creatinine (renal function) and full blood count / CRP
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess hydration/electrolyte status and screen for an inflammatory/infective process contributing to the crash — arrange as part of prompt review; not an emergency in a haemodynamically stable, afebrile patient.
Longitudinal work-up to exclude chronic Q fever endocarditis given the prior C. burnetii infection — belongs in ongoing care, not the acute telehealth contact.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Q fever (Coxiella burnetii) phase I/II serology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterise chronic Q fever status and endocarditis risk as part of ID-guided longitudinal management.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Non-contrast CT / MRI brain for acute stroke work-up
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated on the current screened-negative symmetric, non-focal picture with a clear 2-day exertional trigger; emergency neuroimaging becomes appropriate ONLY if a new focal/lateralising deficit appears (then T5). Reflexively arranging emergency stroke imaging here is over-investigation and drives harmful over-triage.
